channel.title: Notes on Stephen D. Gregory
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on Stephen D. Gregory

item.title: Ordered factors and their analysis
item.description: What is an ordered factor? Why might we use them? And what are the implications of using ordered factors in statistical analysis? If you’d like my views on these questions, then read on… Background What is an ordered factor? An ordered factor is a categorical variable whose levels have a meaningful order. In the oven knob picture above, there are 4 levels: ‘OFF’, ‘LOW’, ‘MED’ and ‘HIGH’ and these should be ordered as ‘OFF < LOW < MED < HIGH’.
